goals.description: Reconnect deeply with my close friend group (cohort_3) tonight to solidify our bond before it drifts, explicitly stating that my upcoming exam and sleep debt prevent me from attending the club social at 21:00.
goals.rationale: My affiliation axis dictates that maintaining deep trust with cohort_3 is more valuable than superficial participation in a low-stakes event. By setting this boundary now with assertiveness, I protect our relationship integrity while demonstrating reliability as a teammate who prioritizes quality connection over quantity of presence.

goals.description: Complete the final 40% of midterm review by 21:30 to ensure uninterrupted focus, securing the academic payoff without burning out before tomorrow's exam.
goals.rationale: With two hours of sleep debt already incurred, my deliberate planning horizon requires me to treat physical stamina as non-negotiable capital. Protecting my health now prevents future failure and ensures I can show up fully rested for both my exams and my relationships later.

goals.description: Complete the final 40% of midterm review by 21:30 to ensure a solid grade without burning out before tomorrow's exam.

Protecting my academic integrity is non-negotiable for my self-respect, but I must set a hard stop time so I don't sacrifice the social obligation later.

goals.description: Decline Mina and Jae's invitation to Echo firmly but kindly at 21:00, explicitly stating I need to prioritize my study schedule to avoid feeling unreliable tomorrow.
goals.rationale: My values weigh relationships heavily, but I cannot let them down if I show up half-asleep or unprepared. Assertiveness is key here; I will not apologize for protecting my energy. If they push back, I will reiterate my boundary once, then stand firm on my decision to honor my commitment to myself first.

goals.description: Attend the club night as planned if invited by others, or simply leave early if the group pressure becomes too intense, ensuring I don't double down on guilt.
goals.rationale: While I value connection, my current stress levels (4) and sleep debt (2 hours) mean I can only sustain one social interaction tonight. It's better to be present and genuine than to stay until exhaustion sets in, which would hurt my health and future productivity more than missing the event itself.
